water_temp_read.read: Water temperature is in a favorable trout range.

light_pressure_read.read: High pressure and low cloud cover make this fishable but technical.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

caution_notes: Bright/high-pressure conditions may make fish cover-oriented and less forgiving.

generated_text.quick_read: The Chattahoochee River offers good but technical trout fishing with ideal water temperatures at 59°F. Recent stocking enhances fly options including eggs and attractor nymphs.
generated_text.best_window: The morning of day 1 is best due to lighter winds, low sky cover, and stable conditions, which match the angler analysis calling for careful drift presentations and smaller natural flies in these technical, high-pressure conditions.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 59°F, flow is 771 cfs, gage height is 1.83 ft, and turbidity is low at 2.3 NTU as of 7/2 evening.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover is low averaging 22%, atmospheric pressure is high at 30.1 inHg with an unknown trend, wind is light from the NE at 1.6 mph with gusts to 10.4 mph, and rain probability is minimal at 16% with no expected rainfall.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will position in riffles, runs, seams, and deeper shaded structure to stay protected and comfortable under bright, high-pressure skies. Fish the recommended flies with careful, less aggressive drifts prioritizing midges, nymphs, and stocked-trout attractors, while noting trout may be less forgiving and more cover-oriented.
